prompt: The Singleton is not thread-safe. What is the bug?
choices: The constructor must be public for Singleton to work
The check-then-act on instance == null is not atomic; two threads can both see null and each create a new instance
Static fields cannot hold object references in Java
getInstance() must be declared synchronized at the class level
explanation: Without synchronization, two threads can both read instance == null simultaneously and each create a separate Singleton instance. Fix: use double-checked locking with volatile, or the initialization-on-demand holder idiom: private static class Holder { static final Singleton INSTANCE = new Singleton(); }
